title: Replace subagent-based extract with a standalone extract tool
description: Extraction is a single-shot transform, not an agentic loop. Replace the generic subagent-based extract with a dedicated `extract` tool in the Pi extension. Design: - Dedicated extract tool, NOT routed through the generic subagent runner. - Performs a SINGLE in-process LLM model call via the pi SDK (pi-coding-agent / pi-ai) — no child pi process, no tool loop. The model just returns the extracted markdown as text and has no filesystem tools. - Input: the archive's index.html + meta.json, inlined in full into the call (as today). - Output path is a REQUIRED tool argument supplied by the OUTER LLM (not derived in code, not a URL slug). The outer LLM chooses a human filename from the article title (natural case/spaces) and places it per the KB's wiki conventions; the extraction skill instructs it how. - After the model returns, the tool: (1) runs asset adoption on the returned markdown (glin-eyn), (2) verifies every /assets/ ref resolves, (3) writes the file to the caller-provided path (creating parents). Adopt + verify BEFORE writing so a broken extraction never lands on disk. Surface adoption/verify failures in the tool result. Prompt changes (extension/agents/extract.md -> the new tool's system prompt): drop file-writing, path-mirroring/slug rules, adoption-via-bash, and mkdir. Keep faithful transcription, frontmatter, and the image embed format ![alt](/assets/.) derived from the archive HTML . Also update templates/skills/extract/SKILL.md: the outer LLM calls the new extract tool (passing archive + chosen output path) instead of the subagent tool; document title-based naming + wiki-convention placement. Open point: revisit frontmatter id/slug rules now that filenames are title-based (align with wiki conventions). ACCEPTANCE: - Extraction runs as its own extension tool via a single in-process model call with no child tools. - Output path comes from the caller (outer LLM); title-based, not slug. - Assets adopted + verified; a failed extraction does not write a broken source file. - Bundled extract agent removed; SKILL.md updated to the new tool.
acceptance_criteria: extract agent + skill instruct writing /assets/<hash>.<ext> embeds derived from the HTML src with no shasum/cp; bash removed from the agent toolset unless still required solely for mkdir (documented); no relative asset paths remain in the guidance

title: Add asset adoption for extracted sources (glin lib)
description:
Mechanical asset adoption, implemented as a glin LIB function called in-process by the extract tool (glin-b9h). No `glin adopt` CLI subcommand for now — detection is covered by glin doctor (glin-1ik); a repair CLI can be added later if a real need appears.

Input: the extracted markdown (in-memory string) plus the source archive directory (raw/.../). For each image embed of the form /assets/<hash>.<ext> in the markdown, locate the backing file in the archive (raw/.../images/<hash>.<ext>; filename equals the content hash after glin-a54, so lookup is by filename) and copy it into assets/<hash>.<ext>.

Dedup + collision safety: if assets/<hash>.<ext> already exists, verify full content matches before treating it as a dedup hit; on mismatch (a real truncated-hash collision) raise an error rather than silently aliasing.

Runs BEFORE the source file is written so a broken extraction never lands (see glin-b9h). Uses the markdown parser (glin-at6) to find refs. Relies on the absolute /assets link convention (glin-tav).

ACCEPTANCE:
- A glin lib function copies every /assets/<hash>.<ext> referenced by an extracted source from its archive into assets/, deduped with full-content verification, erroring on collision.
- No shasum/cp performed by the model; no CLI subcommand added.
acceptance_criteria: After extraction, every /assets/<hash>.<ext> reference in the new source has a matching file in assets/, copied from the archive; dedup verifies full content; collisions error instead of aliasing; no shasum/cp performed by the subagent

title: Add markdown parsing utility for glin tools
description:
Add a small shared library for parsing markdown in glin tools (src/lib/), initially to extract image/asset references (and ideally links) with their positions so tools can inspect and rewrite them reliably.

Motivation: both the asset adoption pass and glin doctor need to find /assets/... references (and later inter-document links) in source/wiki markdown. Rather than ad-hoc regex in each command, bite the bullet on a proper markdown parse now since multiple tools will need it.

Scope for this task:
- Choose an approach (existing dependency vs minimal in-house). NOTE: adding a dependency requires asking first per AGENTS.md — surface options (e.g. remark/mdast, markdown-it) and tradeoffs before pulling one in.
- Provide functions to enumerate image embeds and links (url + alt/text + source position) from a markdown string.
- Cover with unit tests.

Consumers: the asset adoption pass and glin doctor.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable markdown-parsing module in src/lib/ can enumerate image/link references from markdown; dependency choice discussed/approved; unit tests included

title: Name fetched images by content hash in raw/
description: `glin fetch` should name downloaded images by a truncated content hash instead of a URL-derived filename, and rewrite the archive HTML to match. Design: - Compute SHA-256 of each downloaded image, truncate to 16 hex chars (64 bits; effectively collision-free at the expected 10^3-10^5 images per vault, a quarter the length of full sha256). Store as raw/.../images/.. - Rewrite the archive HTML to the relative images/. so the archive still opens offline. - Human-readable image filenames are intentionally dropped (accepted tradeoff). Why: this puts the content hash directly into the archive HTML the extract subagent reads, so adoption becomes an identity copy (images/.ext -> /assets/.ext) with no hash recomputation or transcription of a 64-char string. Root cause of broken image links today is the small extract model couriering a full SHA-256 from shasum output into markdown. Change is localized to createAssetLocalizer in src/lib/fetch.ts (~200-214), replacing the filenameFromUrlPath/uniquifyFilename naming for the images subdir only (styles unaffected). Keep the existing extension-from-content-type logic. Backstop for truncation: when a destination filename already exists, later adoption must verify full content matches before treating it as dedup (see the adoption-pass task); the fetch side just needs deterministic hash naming.
acceptance_criteria: Fetched images are stored as raw/.../images/<16hexhash>.; archive index.html point at those relative paths; styles unaffected; existing fetch tests updated

title: Extraction via minimal glin-owned Pi extension
description: Build extension/ with subagent tool, extract agent prompt, parent skill, packaging, and tests per plan.
close_reason: Implemented minimal glin Pi extension with subagent tool, extract agent (google/gemini-2.5-flash, isolated prompt), parent skill, packaging, and unit tests.

title: Add glin doctor command to detect broken KB references
description:
A read-only validator that scans the KB for broken/inconsistent references and reports them (nonzero exit on problems, for CI and agent use).

Immediate motivating breakage: adopted image links in sources/ of the form /assets/<hash>.<ext> that have no corresponding file in assets/. This happens when the extract subagent mis-transcribes a content hash (it currently couriers a 64-char SHA-256 from shasum output into markdown; see extract agent + glin-hxc/glin-oj0). doctor should flag every asset reference with no backing file, and ideally the reverse.

Leave room to grow into a general KB linter. Candidate future checks:
- Orphaned assets (files in assets/ referenced by nothing).
- Dangling raw: frontmatter paths on source nodes.
- Broken intra-KB links / wikilinks / backlinks.
- Missing required frontmatter keys per templates schema.
- Sources pointing into raw/ instead of assets/ (live nodes must not reference raw/).

Scope this task to the framework + the missing-asset check; file follow-ups for the rest.
acceptance_criteria: Running glin doctor reports source asset links (/assets/<hash>.ext) with no matching file in assets/; exits nonzero when problems found; read-only (no mutations); structured enough to add further checks later

title: Adopt OKF spec, move into AGENTS.md
description:
Adopt the OKF spec, as a base for vault design. Copy relevant parts of the OKF spec into agent instructions, and reference the external OKF spec.

While doing this, move more information about vault layout out of the system prompt and into the AGENTS.md that is discoverable by non-Glin agents. There's some tension here, as conventions that are assumed/required by the Glin tooling need to be locked in (or the tools made more flexible). But we should make the vault structure more obvious to other agents too.

See https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog/blob/main/okf/SPEC.md

title: Change asset linking to absolute, from vault root
description:
OKF and Obsidian both support absolute links relative to vault root. That's a reasonable format for us to adopt here, without causing too much Obsidian lock-in. 

Note this applies to both links between documents and embedded asset paths.

See OKF spec: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog/blob/main/okf/SPEC.md

title: Strip trailing whitespace before collapsing blank lines in fetch HTML
description:
## Problem

`serializeHtml` (used by `glin fetch` when writing archived HTML) calls `collapseBlankLines`, which only collapses runs of three or more consecutive `\n` characters:

```ts
html.replace(/\n{3,}/g, "\n\n")
```

Lines that look blank but contain trailing spaces/tabs (e.g. `"\n   \n   \n"`) do not match, so whitespace-only lines block collapse and leave noisy vertical gaps in archived HTML.

## What to do

In `src/lib/html.ts`, strip trailing whitespace from each line **before** collapsing blank lines. Order matters: strip first, then collapse.

Likely change in `collapseBlankLines` (or a small helper used by `serializeHtml`):

1. Remove trailing spaces/tabs on each line (e.g. `replace(/[ \t]+$/gm, "")`)
2. Then apply the existing `\n{3,}` → `\n\n` collapse

## Acceptance

- Lines with only spaces/tabs become true blank lines and participate in collapse
- Existing behavior for pure-newline runs is unchanged
- Unit tests in `src/lib/html.test.ts` cover: trailing spaces on blank lines, mixed tabs/spaces, and that strip happens before collapse
acceptance_criteria: Trailing whitespace is stripped per line before blank-line collapse; whitespace-only lines collapse like empty lines; tests cover the new behavior
close_reason: Strip trailing whitespace per line before blank-line collapse; tests added

title: Strip generic subagent tool back to minimal (retain for future)
description:
Once extraction moves to its own tool (glin-b9h), the generic `subagent` tool has no consumer — extract was its only agent. Do NOT delete it: the child-pi spawning + JSON event-stream plumbing is worth retaining for a future general-purpose delegate.

Strip it back to a minimal, working generic subagent: remove extract-specific assumptions/wording, keep the isolated child-pi runner and result/usage plumbing. This is NOT the future context-aware (AGENTS.md / skills-aware) delegate — just preserve the minimal generic form so the learnings survive and it stays easy to extend or delete later.

ACCEPTANCE:
- The subagent tool remains registered and functional in a minimal generic form, with no extract-specific coupling.
- The bundled extract agent (extension/agents/extract.md) is no longer required by it.

title: Add glin command to strip HTML noise for LLM extraction
description:
## Problem

The extract skill passes archived `raw/.../index.html` in full to the extract subagent. Archived HTML is faithful and offline-safe (CSP, localized assets, scripts stripped at fetch time), but still contains a lot of structural noise: nav chrome, empty wrapper divs, link-only subtrees, form widgets, SVG decoration, repeated header/footer markup, etc.

That noise inflates token cost and makes it easier for the LLM to latch onto boilerplate instead of main article content.

## Proposal

Add a new glin subcommand (name TBD: `glin strip-html`, `glin simplify-html`, `glin for-extract`, …) that reads an archived HTML file (or stdin) and prints a reduced HTML (or text) representation tuned for LLM extraction — not for browser viewing.

Mechanical filtering only; no readability heuristics or content interpretation. Reuse/extend logic in `src/lib/html.ts` where sensible.

## Candidate filters (discuss at implementation time)

- Strip `<script>`, `<style>`, `<noscript>`, `<template>`, comments (fetch already removes executable scripts; archived pages may still have data scripts, inline style, etc.)
- Drop subtrees with no text-node leaves (empty layout scaffolding)
- Drop or flatten known chrome tags/roles if reliably detectable (`nav`, `header`, `footer`, `aside`, hidden/offscreen containers) — careful with false positives on article sidebars
- Remove non-content attributes (`class`, `style`, `data-*`, event handlers) while keeping `href`, `src`, `alt`, `title` where needed for images/links
- Optionally collapse excessive blank lines (may share `collapseBlankLines` / serialize path)
- Preserve main-content signals the extractor needs: headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, blockquotes, code/pre, figure/img + alt, links

## Integration

- Update `templates/skills/extract/SKILL.md` (and extension extract agent prompt if needed) to pipe archive HTML through the new command instead of inlining raw `index.html` wholesale
- Command should work on existing `raw/` archives without network access

## Relation to existing code

- `glin fetch` + `cleanHtml` optimizes for **offline browser archive** (CSP, asset localization, script stripping)
- `glin read` cleans live HTTP HTML similarly for stdout
- This command optimizes for **LLM extraction input** — different goals; do not overload fetch/read semantics

## Open questions

- Output format: simplified HTML vs HTML-to-markdown-ish text vs both (`--format html|text`)?
- How aggressive can subtree pruning be before we drop real content (e.g. image-only figures, pull quotes in empty wrappers)?
- Should the command accept flags to tune aggressiveness, or one fixed profile?

## Acceptance

- New subcommand documented in `--help`, usable from shell and agent skills
- Operates on a local archived `index.html` path; no KB network fetch required
- Demonstrably reduces token size on a representative noisy page while keeping article body structure
- Extract skill updated to use it (or issue filed as follow-up if scope split)
acceptance_criteria: New glin subcommand strips archived HTML noise for LLM extraction; extract skill can pipe archive HTML through it instead of inlining raw index.html; reduces tokens on a representative page without losing main content structure

title: Add glin asset command to adopt files from raw/ into assets/
description:
Add a mechanical CLI command (e.g. `glin asset <path>` or `glin adopt-asset`) that copies an image or similar file from raw/ into the pooled assets/ store, naming it by content hash (and preserving extension), and prints the resulting assets/ path (JSON or path on stdout). Dedup: if the hash already exists, do not copy again; return the existing path.

This is the mechanical half of eager image adoption during extraction. Needed so the extract subagent can drop the bash tool (see glin-oj0) and call glin instead of shasum/cp for each used image.

title: Consider bundling mkdir and dropping bash from extract subagent
description: The extract agent currently needs bash for mkdir -p (sources/ parents, assets/) and for hash-deduped image adoption (shasum + cp). Consider adding a small mechanical glin (or extension) mkdir/copy-assets primitive so the extract subagent can drop the bash tool and run with a tighter tool set (read, write, and maybe a dedicated adopt-assets helper). Weigh complexity vs. reduced blast radius of giving the child a shell.

title: Support parallel subagents for extraction tasks
description: Extend the glin Pi extension subagent tool to run multiple extract tasks concurrently (like the earendil subagent example's parallel mode). Useful when extracting many freshly fetched raw/ archives in one go. Currently only single {agent, task} is supported.

title: Add parallel fetch with per-domain rate limiting to glin fetch
description:
Agents using glin often fetch many URLs in one session. Today `glin fetch` accepts a single URL and runs sequentially when invoked repeatedly; callers must roll their own shell loops, xargs, or ad-hoc concurrency. That duplicates logic and makes courteous rate limiting inconsistent across agents.

Build first-class parallelism into `glin fetch` so agents can pass multiple URLs (or read a list from stdin/file) and glin schedules work with sensible defaults. Include courtesy configuration for rate limiting requests per source domain — e.g. max concurrent requests per host and/or minimum interval between requests to the same host — so bulk fetches do not hammer a single site.

Scope is mechanical scheduling only: reuse existing `fetchPage` / archive logic; do not add extraction or content interpretation.
design:
- Keep `fetchPage` as the unit of work; add a thin batch orchestrator in `lib/`
- Bucket URLs by registrable domain (reuse `sanitizeDomain` or equivalent) for per-host limits
- Global pool limits total in-flight fetches; per-domain semaphore or token bucket enforces courtesy caps
- CLI flags over config file for now (convention over configuration); promote to KB config only if a second use case appears
- Consider JSON-lines stdout for batch mode vs array — pick one and document; agents parsing stdout need a stable contract
acceptance_criteria:
- `glin fetch` accepts multiple URLs (positional args and/or `--file` / stdin list)
- Concurrent fetches run in parallel with a configurable global concurrency cap
- Per-domain rate limiting is configurable (e.g. `--concurrency-per-domain`, `--domain-delay` or similar); sensible defaults documented in `--help`
- Each URL still produces the existing JSON result shape on stdout; failures for one URL do not abort the whole batch unless `--fail-fast` is set
- Progress and per-domain throttling logged to stderr
- Tests cover scheduling, domain bucketing, and rate-limit behavior (mock HTTP)
notes: Motivation: fetch-articles skill and similar agent workflows batch many pages; parallelism belongs in the tool per AGENTS.md division of labor (glin = mechanical, agent = judgment).
